Through research, collaboration, professional engagement, and thought leadership, fellows will play an important role in advancing discussions on future warfare and statecraft.

Applications are currently open and will close on June 30, 2026.

About the Institute for Future Conflict

The Institute for Future Conflict (IFC) is based at the United States Air Force Academy and exists to advance understanding and preparation for future conflict through research, education, leadership development, and external engagement.

The institute focuses on helping develop adaptive military leaders capable of responding effectively to evolving global security challenges. Its work examines a wide range of topics, including:

  • Emerging technologies and warfare
  • Strategic competition among nations
  • Adversary doctrine and military innovation
  • Force design and military modernization
  • Operational adaptation
  • Defense policy and strategy
  • The relationship between military power and society
  • Future operational environments
  • National security challenges

Through its partnerships with government agencies, academic institutions, military organizations, and industry leaders, the institute seeks to develop innovative approaches to future security challenges.

About the Nonresident Research Fellowship

The Nonresident Research Fellowship is an annual program that runs alongside the academic year and lasts for one calendar year.

The fellowship brings together individuals from diverse backgrounds who share a common interest in understanding and shaping the future of conflict and strategic competition.

Rather than following a rigid structure, the fellowship is designed to be flexible and tailored to each fellow’s expertise, research interests, and professional goals.

Successful applicants will work closely with the Fellowship Director to create individualized engagement plans that maximize their contributions while supporting their own professional development.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive several valuable benefits throughout the fellowship period.

$2,000 Honorarium

Each fellow receives a $2,000 honorarium for active participation in the program and contributions to the intellectual life of the Institute for Future Conflict.

Travel Support

The institute covers travel expenses associated with required fellowship events and workshops.

Access to High-Level Networks

Fellows gain access to a unique network of:

  • Military leaders
  • Government officials
  • Policy professionals
  • Academic researchers
  • Defense experts
  • Industry innovators
  • Former and current fellows

Research and Publication Opportunities

Participants have opportunities to:

  • Publish original research
  • Develop policy analysis papers
  • Produce strategic commentary
  • Contribute to ongoing debates on national security and future warfare

Professional Development

The fellowship provides valuable opportunities to:

  • Refine research agendas
  • Expand professional networks
  • Engage with defense audiences
  • Develop publication pipelines
  • Collaborate with leading experts

Fellowship Activities

The fellowship offers flexibility in how participants contribute.

Examples of fellowship activities include:

Research and Writing

Fellows may:

  • Conduct original research
  • Produce policy papers
  • Write strategic analyses
  • Publish commentary on future conflict topics

Collaborative Projects

Participants can collaborate with IFC-affiliated scholars on:

  • Research projects
  • Publications
  • Applied defense initiatives
  • Strategic studies

Presentations and Public Engagement

Fellows may:

  • Deliver lectures and presentations
  • Engage military audiences
  • Brief policymakers
  • Speak to industry stakeholders
  • Participate in public discussions

Cadet Mentorship

Participants can contribute to educational initiatives by:

  • Supporting cadet research projects
  • Advising student researchers
  • Participating in Future Conflict Minor activities

Workshops and Wargames

Fellows are encouraged to engage in:

  • Strategic workshops
  • Wargaming exercises
  • Speaker series
  • Research forums

Required Fellowship Deliverable

Each fellow must produce at least one written product for publication on the Institute for Future Conflict website.

This publication may include:

  • Research articles
  • Policy analysis
  • Strategic commentary
  • Defense-focused essays
  • Future warfare assessments

Fellowship Timeline

August 21, 2026

Selected fellows will attend a kickoff workshop at the United States Air Force Academy.

This workshop serves as an opportunity to:

  • Present project ideas
  • Receive feedback from peers and experts
  • Establish research goals
  • Set publication deadlines

Summer 2027

Fellows will reconvene to present their completed research and share insights gained during the fellowship year.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship welcomes applicants from a variety of professional backgrounds.

Early-Career Scholars

Doctoral candidates and emerging researchers can use the fellowship to:

  • Build academic networks
  • Strengthen research portfolios
  • Develop publication opportunities

Mid-Career and Senior Scholars

Experienced researchers may use the fellowship to:

  • Engage defense audiences
  • Influence policy discussions
  • Expand professional collaborations

Practitioners and Professionals

Military personnel, government officials, analysts, and industry professionals can leverage the fellowship to:

  • Connect practical experience with research
  • Contribute operational insights
  • Shape future defense thinking

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the fellowship, applicants must:

Citizenship Requirement

  • Be a United States citizen

Education Requirement

Applicants must possess:

  • A master’s degree or higher; OR
  • Equivalent professional experience

Research Focus Requirement

Applicants must demonstrate a strong focus on:

  • Future conflict
  • Strategic competition
  • National security
  • Defense studies
  • Military innovation
  • Related fields

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following materials:

1. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

A comprehensive CV outlining educational background, research experience, publications, and professional accomplishments.

2. Cover Letter

A one- to two-page cover letter explaining:

  • Professional background
  • Alignment with the Institute for Future Conflict
  • Proposed fellowship project
  • Expected contributions to the program

3. Writing Sample

Applicants must provide a previously published writing sample demonstrating their research, analytical, and communication abilities.

The Institute emphasizes originality and expects all submitted materials to accurately reflect the applicant’s own work.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted via email.

Send application materials to:

Dr. Kerry Chávez
kerry.chavez@afacademy.af.edu

CC:

Dr. Layne Hancock
cary.hancock@afacademy.af.edu

Apply Here

Application Deadline

June 30, 2026

Applicants should ensure that all required documents are submitted before the close of business on the deadline date.

Why This Fellowship Matters

As emerging technologies, geopolitical competition, artificial intelligence, cyber warfare, and evolving military doctrines continue to reshape the global security landscape, understanding future conflict has become more important than ever.

The Institute for Future Conflict Nonresident Research Fellowship offers a unique platform for experts and emerging leaders to contribute meaningful research, influence strategic thinking, and engage directly with military and policy communities shaping the future of national and international security.

For scholars, practitioners, and professionals interested in the future of warfare and strategic competition, this fellowship represents a valuable opportunity to advance both personal expertise and broader discussions on global security.

For more information on this fellowship visit here

For more fellowship opportunities on OFY click here  

The post Institute for Future Conflict Nonresident Research Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers, Scholars, and Strategic Thinkers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ACET Youth Policy Lab 2026: Fully Virtual Pan-African Policy Innovation Challenge for Young Leaders Driving Africa’s Future
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The African Center for Economic Transformation (ACET) has officially opened applications for the ACET Youth Policy Lab (AYPL) 2026, a unique continent-wide policy innovation challenge designed to place young Africans at the center of policymaking, innovation, and economic transformation. Under the theme, “From Coordination to Commercialization: Youth, Innovation & Growth,” the 2026 edition invites ambitious […]

The post ACET Youth Policy Lab 2026: Fully Virtual Pan-African Policy Innovation Challenge for Young Leaders Driving Africa’s Future appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Center for Economic Transformation (ACET) has officially opened applications for the ACET Youth Policy Lab (AYPL) 2026, a unique continent-wide policy innovation challenge designed to place young Africans at the center of policymaking, innovation, and economic transformation.

Under the theme, “From Coordination to Commercialization: Youth, Innovation & Growth,” the 2026 edition invites ambitious young Africans to step into the role of policymakers and tackle some of Africa’s most pressing development challenges through evidence-based policy design, collaboration, mentorship, and leadership development.

The ACET Youth Policy Lab is much more than a competition. It is an immersive learning experience that combines the excitement of a hackathon with the rigor of real-world policy development, equipping participants with practical skills in governance, innovation, leadership, and policy analysis.

For young Africans who aspire to influence public policy, drive innovation, and contribute to sustainable development across the continent, this opportunity offers a powerful platform to learn, collaborate, and create impact.

What is the ACET Youth Policy Lab?

The ACET Youth Policy Lab is a simulation-based policy innovation platform that empowers young Africans to engage directly with policymaking processes.

Participants are placed within a fictional institution known as the National Youth Transformation Authority (NYTA), where they assume the role of decision-makers responsible for developing solutions to real-world development challenges facing African countries.

The program is specifically designed to:

  • Strengthen youth participation in policymaking
  • Develop policy analysis and problem-solving skills
  • Promote innovation-driven development solutions
  • Enhance leadership capabilities
  • Foster collaboration among young Africans
  • Increase youth representation in governance spaces

Unlike traditional competitions, participants are challenged to think critically about policy trade-offs, institutional constraints, and implementation realities.

Theme for 2026: From Coordination to Commercialization – Youth, Innovation & Growth

The 2026 Youth Policy Lab focuses on one of Africa’s most important development priorities: transforming innovative ideas into sustainable economic growth opportunities.

Participants will explore how effective policy coordination, innovation ecosystems, and commercialization strategies can create jobs, strengthen industries, and accelerate Africa’s economic transformation.

The theme encourages young leaders to design practical solutions that support inclusive growth while addressing systemic barriers that often limit innovation and entrepreneurship across the continent.

Policy Challenges Participants Will Address

Through the simulation, participants will work on policy challenges connected to:

1. Youth Employment and Skills Development

Participants will explore policies that can:

  • Reduce youth unemployment
  • Improve workforce readiness
  • Strengthen technical and vocational education
  • Enhance employability and entrepreneurship opportunities

2. Innovation and Commercialization

Teams will examine strategies for:

  • Supporting innovators and startups
  • Commercializing research and innovation
  • Building stronger innovation ecosystems
  • Scaling locally developed solutions

3. Digital Transformation

Participants will develop ideas related to:

  • Digital inclusion
  • Emerging technologies
  • Digital entrepreneurship
  • Technology-driven public services

4. Governance and Institutional Reform

The program challenges participants to consider:

  • Effective governance structures
  • Public sector reforms
  • Accountability systems
  • Institutional efficiency

5. Financing and Donor Coordination

Teams will analyze ways to:

  • Mobilize development financing
  • Improve donor coordination
  • Strengthen resource allocation
  • Increase investment effectiveness

Experience Real-World Policymaking

One of the most exciting aspects of the ACET Youth Policy Lab is its realism.

Participants will not simply generate ideas; they will navigate the same challenges policymakers face every day, including:

  • Limited national budgets
  • IMF restrictions
  • Political pressures
  • Competing stakeholder interests
  • Resource constraints
  • Implementation challenges

This approach ensures participants gain practical experience in balancing ambition with reality.

Why You Should Apply

The ACET Youth Policy Lab offers a wide range of professional and leadership benefits.

Build Practical Policy Skills

Participants gain hands-on experience in:

  • Policy analysis
  • Research
  • Strategic thinking
  • Public problem-solving
  • Evidence-based policymaking

Receive Expert Mentorship

Selected participants will receive guidance from leading African experts and practitioners.

Mentors will help teams refine ideas, strengthen proposals, and improve policy recommendations.

Collaborate Across Africa

Participants will work with talented young leaders from different countries and backgrounds.

This cross-cultural collaboration strengthens teamwork and exposes participants to diverse perspectives.

Develop Leadership Skills

The program helps participants enhance:

  • Communication skills
  • Decision-making abilities
  • Negotiation techniques
  • Leadership capacity

Create a Policy Proposal

Each team will develop a comprehensive three-page policy proposal that addresses a critical development challenge.

Pitch Your Ideas

Participants will present their solutions during a live virtual pitch event before experts and judges.

Gain Access to Future Opportunities

The program offers pathways to:

  • Internship opportunities
  • Ambassador roles
  • Professional mentorship
  • Regional visibility
  • Recognition across ACET platforms

Additional Benefits for Winning Participants

Winning participants will receive:

  • Cash vouchers
  • Internship opportunities
  • Mentorship support
  • Certificates of participation and achievement
  • Regional recognition through ACET networks and platforms

These rewards are designed to support participants’ continued growth and leadership development.

How the ACET Youth Policy Lab Works

The program follows a structured journey that combines learning, collaboration, coaching, and competition.

Step 1: Application Stage

Young Africans submit their applications and demonstrate their interest in policy innovation and leadership.

Step 2: Team Formation

Selected applicants are grouped into six teams, each consisting of five participants.

Step 3: Mentorship and Coaching

Teams participate in mentorship sessions and receive guidance from experts.

Step 4: Policy Development

Participants collaborate to design evidence-based policy solutions and prepare their proposals.

Step 5: Virtual Pitch Event

Teams present their recommendations during the final virtual pitch competition.

Step 6: Winners Announcement

Top-performing teams are recognized and rewarded.

Step 7: YES Ambassador Program

Participants may have opportunities to join a regional youth network through the YES Ambassador Program.

Eligibility Criteria

To apply, candidates must meet the following requirements:

Age Requirement

Applicants must be between 18 and 35 years old.

Citizenship Requirement

Applicants must be citizens of African countries.

Language Requirement

Applicants must be fluent in either:

  • English
  • French

Commitment Requirement

Applicants must be able to dedicate approximately 4–6 hours weekly throughout the program.

Important Dates

Applications Open

15 June 2026

Application Deadline

7 July 2026

Team Shortlisting

8 July – 31 July 2026

Team Interviews

1 August – 31 August 2026

Participant Onboarding

1 September – 18 September 2026

Coaching and Mentorship Period

21 September – 16 October 2026

Grand Finale and Virtual Pitch Event

4 November 2026

Winners Announcement

Following the final pitch event.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the Online Application Form through the official ACET Youth Policy Lab portal.

For inquiries:
youthpolicylab@acetforafrica.org

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully review all program information before submitting their applications.

Why This Opportunity Matters for Africa’s Future

Africa has the world’s youngest population, making youth participation in policymaking essential for sustainable development.

The ACET Youth Policy Lab recognizes that young people are not merely beneficiaries of policy decisions; they are innovators, entrepreneurs, problem-solvers, and future leaders capable of driving transformative change.

By equipping young Africans with practical policymaking experience, leadership training, and access to expert mentorship, the program contributes to building a stronger generation of leaders who can shape inclusive and prosperous futures across the continent.

Visit HERE to learn more about ACET Youth Policy Lab 2026.

Conclusion

The ACET Youth Policy Lab 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious young Africans to gain hands-on experience in policymaking, innovation, leadership, and economic transformation. Through collaboration, mentorship, policy simulation, and competitive pitching, participants will develop the skills needed to influence real-world change and contribute meaningfully to Africa’s development agenda.

For young leaders passionate about governance, innovation, entrepreneurship, public policy, and sustainable development, this program offers a unique platform to learn from experts, connect with peers across Africa, and transform ideas into impactful policy solutions. Interested candidates should submit their applications before the 7 July 2026 deadline and take the next step toward becoming part of Africa’s next generation of changemakers.

Discover more global African, Leadership and Policy Development opportunities on OFY.

The post ACET Youth Policy Lab 2026: Fully Virtual Pan-African Policy Innovation Challenge for Young Leaders Driving Africa’s Future appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WHO Youth Council 2026–2028: Global Call for Youth Organizations to Shape International Health Policy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The World Health Organization (WHO) has officially launched a global call for expressions of interest for the third term of the WHO Youth Council (2026–2028). This exciting opportunity invites youth-led and youth-focused organizations from around the world to play a direct role in influencing global health discussions, policies, and strategies. As one of WHO’s flagship […]

The post WHO Youth Council 2026–2028: Global Call for Youth Organizations to Shape International Health Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Health Organization (WHO) has officially launched a global call for expressions of interest for the third term of the WHO Youth Council (2026–2028). This exciting opportunity invites youth-led and youth-focused organizations from around the world to play a direct role in influencing global health discussions, policies, and strategies.

As one of WHO’s flagship youth engagement platforms, the WHO Youth Council provides an avenue for young people and youth organizations to contribute meaningfully to international health governance. Through advocacy, collaboration, innovation, and policy engagement, selected organizations will help strengthen youth participation in shaping healthier and more resilient communities worldwide.

Organizations interested in becoming part of this influential network are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline and carefully review all eligibility requirements.

About the WHO Youth Council

The WHO Youth Council was established to strengthen youth engagement within the World Health Organization by creating a structured platform through which young people can contribute to health-related decision-making processes.

The Council serves multiple purposes, including:

  • Amplifying youth voices in global health discussions
  • Providing insights from young people on emerging health challenges
  • Supporting WHO’s health promotion and advocacy initiatives
  • Encouraging innovative youth-led solutions to public health issues
  • Strengthening collaboration between youth organizations and international health stakeholders
  • Promoting youth participation in health systems and policy development

The Council reflects WHO’s commitment to ensuring that young people are not merely beneficiaries of health policies but active contributors to the design and implementation of solutions that affect their lives and communities.

Why the WHO Youth Council Matters

Young people represent a significant proportion of the global population and are increasingly recognized as critical stakeholders in addressing public health challenges.

Through the WHO Youth Council, participating organizations can:

Influence Global Health Policies

Council members have opportunities to provide perspectives and recommendations that inform WHO strategies and initiatives.

Elevate Youth Priorities

Organizations can ensure that issues affecting young people are represented in international health conversations.

Strengthen Global Networks

Members gain access to a diverse network of youth organizations, health advocates, experts, and policymakers from around the world.

Promote Youth Leadership

The Council provides a platform for organizations to demonstrate leadership and advocate for meaningful youth participation in health governance.

Contribute Innovative Solutions

Organizations can share innovative approaches and experiences that support stronger and more inclusive health systems.

Who Is WHO Looking For?

WHO is seeking a diverse and representative group of organizations from across the globe.

Eligible organizations include:

1. Youth-Led Organizations

Organizations where young people hold leadership positions and drive decision-making processes.

2. Youth-Focused Organizations

Organizations that primarily work to support, empower, or advocate for young people.

3. Youth Chapters of Larger Organizations

Youth branches or youth wings operating within larger national or international organizations.

WHO has also indicated its interest in selecting a balanced mix of organizations from both:

  • Health-related sectors
  • Non-health sectors

This multidisciplinary approach ensures diverse perspectives and strengthens the Council’s ability to address complex public health challenges.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for membership, organizations must satisfy all requirements outlined in the revised WHO Youth Council Terms of Reference.

Key eligibility expectations include:

  • Active engagement at the international level
  • Demonstrated commitment to youth engagement and participation
  • Alignment with WHO values and objectives
  • Capacity to contribute meaningfully to Council activities
  • Compliance with WHO membership requirements

Applicants are strongly encouraged to thoroughly review the Terms of Reference before submitting their application.

WHO Civil Society Commission Requirement

An important requirement for participation in the WHO Youth Council is membership in the WHO Civil Society Commission.

WHO has announced that all Youth Council members will also be expected to participate in the WHO Civil Society Commission.

Organizations that are not already members must:

  1. Submit an application to join the WHO Civil Society Commission.
  2. Complete this step before submitting their WHO Youth Council expression of interest.

WHO has clarified that applications to the Youth Council can still be considered while the organization’s Civil Society Commission application is under review.

This requirement aims to strengthen collaboration between youth organizations and broader civil society stakeholders engaged in global health initiatives.

Application Process

Organizations wishing to apply must complete all required application components.

Step 1: Complete the WHO Youth Council Application Form

Applicants must fill out the official online application form and provide all requested information.

Step 2: Submit the Tobacco-Arms Disclosure Form

In addition to the application form, organizations must complete and submit the Tobacco-Arms Disclosure Form.

Step 3: Ensure Compliance with Eligibility Requirements

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  • Compliance with eligibility criteria
  • Organizational experience and track record
  • Motivation for joining the Council
  • Proposed plans and contributions
  • Diversity and representation considerations

Organizations should provide clear and compelling information demonstrating how they can contribute to the Council’s mission and objectives.

APPLY HERE

Selection Criteria

WHO will assess applications using several factors, including:

Organizational Relevance

The organization’s work and activities should align with youth engagement and meaningful participation.

Experience

Applicants should demonstrate relevant experience in advocacy, leadership, public health, youth development, community engagement, or related areas.

Motivation

Organizations should clearly explain why they wish to join the Council and how they plan to contribute.

Diversity

WHO seeks broad geographic, thematic, and organizational representation to ensure that multiple perspectives are reflected within the Council.

Important Dates

Application Deadline

30 June 2026 at 23:59 CEST

Organizations are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical challenges.

First In-Person Meeting

October 2026

Selected organizations will participate in the Council’s first in-person meeting, marking the beginning of the 2026–2028 term.

Documents Applicants Must Review

Before applying, organizations should carefully read and complete the following documents:

  1. WHO Youth Council Terms of Reference
  2. Tobacco-Arms Disclosure Form

Reviewing these materials is essential for understanding the Council’s responsibilities, expectations, and operational framework.

Benefits of Joining the WHO Youth Council

Participation in the WHO Youth Council offers several valuable advantages:

  • Opportunity to engage directly with WHO
  • Influence international health policy discussions
  • Amplify youth voices at the global level
  • Collaborate with youth organizations worldwide
  • Strengthen advocacy and leadership capacity
  • Expand international partnerships and networks
  • Contribute to global public health initiatives
  • Participate in high-level dialogues and consultations
  • Increase organizational visibility within the global health ecosystem
  • Support meaningful youth participation in health systems strengthening

How to Prepare a Strong Application

Organizations seeking selection should consider the following strategies:

Demonstrate Impact

Highlight measurable achievements and successful initiatives led by your organization.

Showcase Youth Leadership

Clearly explain how young people participate in leadership and decision-making processes.

Emphasize Global Engagement

Illustrate international partnerships, collaborations, or activities that demonstrate global reach.

Present Clear Goals

Explain how your organization intends to contribute to the Council’s work during the 2026–2028 term.

Align with WHO Priorities

Demonstrate a commitment to health equity, inclusion, youth participation, and sustainable development.

Interested organizations are required to complete the online WHO Youth Council Application Form.

Click HERE to learn more about the WHO Youth Council 2026–2028.

Conclusion

The WHO Youth Council 2026–2028 presents an exceptional opportunity for youth-led and youth-focused organizations to engage directly with one of the world’s most influential health institutions. Through this platform, organizations can help shape health policies, advocate for youth priorities, contribute innovative ideas, and strengthen youth participation in global health governance.

Organizations from around the world that are passionate about advancing youth engagement and improving public health outcomes should take advantage of this opportunity and submit their applications before the 30 June 2026 deadline. Participation in the WHO Youth Council offers not only global visibility and networking opportunities but also a meaningful chance to influence the future of international health policy and practice.

Discover more global Fellowship, Health and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY.

The post WHO Youth Council 2026–2028: Global Call for Youth Organizations to Shape International Health Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
